Title: Lock packaging target and wheel strategy
Description: Define the release-track packaging strategy for platform-specific wheels that bundle the Go engine, and align docs/config around that strategy
Acceptance: Wheel strategy is explicit in repo docs/config, packaged binary path is fixed as `markmaton/bin/markmaton-engine*`, and the release track does not rely on `py3-none-any` wheels
Test_Method: manual: review `pyproject.toml`, `docs/packaging-layout.md`, and the release-readiness plan for a single coherent packaging strategy

Title: Implement Hatch build hook for bundled Go binaries
Description: Add a build hook that stages or builds the Go engine and injects it into wheel builds without changing the runtime Python wrapper contract
Acceptance: A custom Hatch build hook exists, wheel builds are marked platform-specific, and the hook bundles `markmaton/bin/markmaton-engine*` into wheel output
Test_Method: manual: inspect `hatch_build.py` and run `python3 -m build --wheel` once build dependencies are available

Title: Wire packaging metadata for wheel and sdist targets
Description: Update `pyproject.toml` so wheel and sdist targets include the right Python package files, Go sources, and build-hook support for release builds
Acceptance: `pyproject.toml` contains explicit wheel/sdist configuration that supports bundled binaries and source distribution without polluting runtime metadata
Test_Method: manual: inspect target config and run `python3 -m build --sdist --wheel` once build dependencies are available
Notes: No parser-scope feature changes in this row

Title: Add Python boundary coverage for packaged-binary discovery
Description: Extend Python unit coverage so packaged-binary discovery and explicit-path precedence are protected before release work relies on them
Acceptance: Python unit tests cover packaged binary lookup behavior and still protect explicit path / env override semantics
Notes: Unit-test only; do not add live install tests here

Title: Replace placeholder workflow with CI workflow
Description: Replace the placeholder GitHub workflow with a real CI workflow that runs Go tests, Python unit tests, and a local-dev smoke build on ordinary pushes and PRs
Acceptance: `.github/workflows/ci.yml` exists, the placeholder workflow is removed or superseded, and CI covers Go tests, Python unit tests, and a repo-local binary smoke path
Test_Method: manual: inspect `ci.yml` trigger/steps and run the same commands locally
Notes: Keep CI separate from publish workflow

Title: Add release workflow for wheels, sdist, and artifacts
Description: Create a dedicated release workflow that builds platform wheels and sdist, uploads artifacts, and separates build from publish jobs
Acceptance: `.github/workflows/workflow.yml` exists, build and publish are separate jobs, wheel/sdist artifacts are uploaded, and publish is only reachable from tag or manual release triggers
Test_Method: manual: inspect `workflow.yml` for matrix/build-artifact flow and stable workflow filename
Notes: Release workflow filename must stay stable for Trusted Publishing

Title: Configure Trusted Publishing workflow contract
Description: Align workflow permissions, environment names, and docs with PyPI/TestPyPI Trusted Publishing so the repo is ready for OIDC-based publishing
Acceptance: Publish jobs use `permissions: id-token: write`, environment names are documented, and release docs explain the exact Trusted Publisher binding needed
Test_Method: manual: inspect release workflow and release docs against PyPI Trusted Publishing requirements

Title: Add install-state smoke path for built wheels
Description: Add a focused install-state smoke path that validates the packaged CLI can locate the bundled binary after wheel installation
Acceptance: A smoke script or documented command exists that installs a built wheel into a clean environment and verifies `markmaton convert` works against a small HTML sample
Test_Method: manual: run the documented install-state smoke path with a built wheel
Notes: This is release validation, not default unit coverage

Title: Update release-facing documentation
Description: Add or update release docs so repository users know how CI, release builds, TestPyPI/PyPI publishing, and local smoke fit together
Acceptance: `README.md` and release docs explain the packaging/release path clearly enough for a maintainer to follow without guessing
Test_Method: manual: review `README.md`, `docs/packaging-layout.md`, and `docs/pypi-release.md` for a coherent release story
Notes: Docs should reflect the actual workflow filenames

Title: Build and inspect release artifacts locally
Description: Once packaging code is in place and build dependencies are available, build local wheel/sdist artifacts and verify the bundled binary layout
Acceptance: Local `dist/` artifacts build successfully, wheel contents include `markmaton/bin/markmaton-engine*`, and no wheel claims `py3-none-any` incorrectly
Test_Method: python3 -m build --sdist --wheel --no-isolation | manual: inspect `dist/*` filenames and wheel contents
Notes: Validated locally with a temporary build environment and wheel inspection

Title: Run full regression and release-readiness checks
Description: After packaging and workflows are in place, rerun Go/Python tests and confirm the release docs/workflows/artifacts are internally consistent
Acceptance: Go tests pass, Python unit tests pass, and the release-readiness path is coherent end to end without changing parser behavior
Test_Method: env GOCACHE=/tmp/markmaton-go-build GOMODCACHE=/tmp/markmaton-go-mod go test ./... | PYTHONPYCACHEPREFIX=/tmp/markmaton-pyc python3 -m unittest discover -s tests -p 'test_*.py' | manual: inspect docs/workflows/artifacts

Title: Validate release-readiness planning artifacts
Description: Validate that the release-readiness plan and issue CSV are well-formed and aligned with the intended packaging epic
Acceptance: The plan file exists, this CSV validates, and the issue ordering matches the release-readiness execution chain
